industry: Precision Photonics, Motion and Vision / Scientific and Technical Instruments
thesis: Novanta is a precision-technology enabler that supplies photonics (laser beam steering, sources), precision motion, and machine-vision subsystems to medical and advanced-industrial OEMs — the picks-and-shovels inside surgical robots, DNA sequencers, semiconductor tools, and factory automation. It runs two segments (Automation Enabling Technologies, ~51%, and Medical Solutions, ~49%), with medical end markets ~53% of sales and growing. FY2025 revenue rose 3% to $981M with adjusted EPS of $3.29, and momentum is re-accelerating: Q1 2026 bookings grew 37% (1.10 book-to-bill) and management raised FY2026 guidance to $1.04-1.055B revenue and $3.50-3.65 adjusted EPS. The $1.45B Riverpoint Medical acquisition pushes Novanta deeper into recurring surgical consumables. The catch is valuation and risk: at ~$149 the stock trades ~40x forward (and ~105x depressed GAAP) earnings, and the debt-and-equity-funded Riverpoint deal adds leverage, dilution, and integration risk. High-quality compounder, full price — hence HOLD, with ~18% upside to the ~$175-180 analyst target if execution holds.

drivers: Riverpoint Medical integrates cleanly and accretes, accelerating high-margin recurring surgical-consumables revenue (medical consumables already ~15% of sales, growing double digits)
Bookings strength (+37% YoY, 1.10 book-to-bill) converts into sustained double-digit organic revenue growth across medical and automation
Automation Enabling Technologies rides robotics, machine vision, and AI-linked precision-motion demand as factory and lab automation expands
Adjusted EBITDA margins expand toward the mid-20s on mix shift to recurring revenue and intelligent subsystems
The market re-rates Novanta as a durable, recurring-revenue precision-tech compounder rather than a cyclical component supplier

base_case.assumptions: Novanta delivers its raised FY2026 guide (revenue $1.04-1.055B, adjusted EPS $3.50-3.65) on mid-to-high-single-digit organic growth plus the Riverpoint contribution, with steady margin expansion. The Riverpoint integration proceeds without major surprises, and the stock holds a premium ~38-42x forward multiple, drifting toward the ~$175-180 analyst targets over the next year as growth is delivered.

risks: Riverpoint integration missteps, higher leverage, and share dilution weigh on returns and the multiple
Advanced-industrial and semiconductor end-market cyclicality slows bookings and revenue
The premium ~40x forward multiple compresses toward instrument/industrial peers on any growth or execution wobble
GAAP earnings stay pressured by acquisition amortization and integration costs, keeping the headline P/E optically extreme
A broad small/mid-cap or industrials de-rating hits a high-beta (~1.7) serial acquirer

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — bookings/book-to-bill, organic growth, and early Riverpoint integration commentary (guide: revenue $259-264M, adj EPS $0.81-0.86)
Riverpoint Medical ($1.45B) closing and integration — surgical-consumables recurring-revenue ramp and synergy delivery
Execution against raised FY2026 guidance (revenue $1.04-1.055B, adjusted EPS $3.50-3.65)
Automation Enabling Technologies demand in robotics, machine vision, and semiconductor/lab automation; deleveraging progress post-acquisition
Q4/FY2026 results and initial FY2027 outlook, including full Riverpoint contribution

key_risks: Full valuation: ~40x forward and ~105x trailing (GAAP) earnings leave limited room for error
Acquisition risk: the $1.45B Riverpoint deal adds leverage, dilution (a $300M private placement plus share registration), and integration risk
Cyclicality in advanced-industrial and semiconductor end markets (~47% of sales)
GAAP earnings depressed by acquisition amortization and integration costs, widening the adjusted-vs-GAAP gap
Customer and OEM concentration; Novanta is a component/subsystem supplier dependent on its OEM customers' own cycles
High beta (~1.7) and small/mid-cap liquidity make the stock sensitive to risk-off rotations
